title: Irish Mob Drama Creator Knew Dropkick Murphys’ ‘Dropped on My Head’ Was Perfect Title Theme: ‘Rowdy Violent Music For a Rowdy Violent Show’
summary: MGM+ series The Westies premieres on Sunday night (July 12).

fullArticle:
If you’re making a TV series about one of New York City’s most notoriously violent Irish gangs, the Dropkick Murphys are gonna want to know about it. The veteran Boston-bred Irish-American Celtic punk group got wind that Narcos creator Chris Brancato was working on a new series for MGM+ called The Westies — inspired by the organized crime gang that terrorized Manhattan’s Hell’s Kitchen neighborhood in the early 1960s — and they wanted in.

“Rowdy violent music for a rowdy violent show. Seems like a perfect match,” Murphys singer Ken Casey tells Billboard about why he and the crew thought their rollicking, felonious anthem “Dropped on My Head,” would be the perfect main title theme song for the series.
